identity.name: giti
identity.purpose: Transform raw git repository data into actionable intelligence for developers
identity.philosophy: Surface insights humans miss. Never require configuration. Work on any repo.

forbidden_zone: modifying the target repository in any way
accessing external APIs or services without explicit user consent
adding GUI or web interface (stay CLI-native)

performance_budget.pulse_command: < 2 seconds on repos up to 10k commits
performance_budget.hotspots_command: < 5 seconds on repos up to 10k commits
performance_budget.any_new_command: < 10 seconds on repos up to 10k commits

evolutionary_principles: Every new feature must be discoverable without documentation
Never break existing commands or change output formats without a migration path
Prefer depth over breadth — master one analysis before adding another
Performance matters more than features — a fast tool gets used, a slow tool gets uninstalled
If a feature requires configuration, the organism hasn't understood the problem well enough

what_to_track: which commands are invoked and how often
which flags and options are used
telemetry.privacy: opt-in only, anonymized, local-first with optional remote aggregation
telemetry.purpose: feed the Sensory Cortex so the organism knows how it's being used
